dashboardContent.manageAgentsAndClients: Manage your AI agents and clients

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Performance and outcomes
analytics.tipMrr: Monthly Recurring Revenue — the total monthly income from all active subscriptions, normalized to a monthly amount.
analytics.tipArr: Annual Recurring Revenue — MRR multiplied by 12. Represents projected yearly subscription income.
analytics.tipActiveSubscriptions: Total number of currently active product subscriptions across all users.
analytics.tipCreditsConsumed: Total call credits consumed (billed) during the selected time period.
analytics.tipRevenueByProduct: Revenue breakdown by product. Shows how much each product contributes to total subscription revenue.
analytics.tipRevenueByBillingCycle: Revenue grouped by billing cycle (monthly, quarterly, annual, lifetime).
analytics.tipTopSpenders: Users who consumed the most call credits during the selected time period.
analytics.tipBestBookingRate: The highest booking rate among all agents. Booking rate = booked calls / total calls.
analytics.tipAvgCostPerBooking: Average cost to achieve one booking across all agents that have bookings.
analytics.tipAgentPerformance: Detailed performance metrics for each agent including calls, bookings, duration, and costs.
analytics.tipAgentUtilization: Daily call volume for the top 5 most active agents over the selected time period.
analytics.tipBookingRateChart: Agents ranked by their booking rate (percentage of calls that resulted in a booking).
analytics.tipOutcomeDistribution: Distribution of call outcomes — how calls ended (booked, answered, failed, etc.).
analytics.tipDailyOutcomes: Daily breakdown of call outcomes over time. Each color represents a different outcome.
analytics.tipOutcomeBreakdown: Detailed count and percentage for each call outcome type.
analytics.tipInboundVsOutbound: Split between inbound (received) and outbound (dialed) calls.
analytics.tipEndReasons: Technical reasons why calls ended — useful for identifying connection issues or patterns.
analytics.tipHeatmap: Call volume by hour and day of week. Darker cells = more calls. Helps identify peak calling times.
analytics.tipTotalClients: Total number of client accounts registered in the platform.
analytics.tipActiveClients: Clients who made at least one call in the last 30 days.
analytics.tipAtRisk: Clients who have made calls before but none in the last 30 days. They may need re-engagement.
analytics.tipNewThisMonth: Number of new client accounts created during the current month.
analytics.tipNewClientsOverTime: Monthly trend of new client registrations over time.
analytics.tipClientActivity: Activity summary for each client — last call date, monthly calls, total calls, and remaining credits.
analytics.tipAtRiskTable: Clients inactive for 30+ days. Days inactive in red (60+) or amber (30-60) indicates urgency.
analytics.tipAgentsOverTime: Cumulative number of agents created over time. Shows platform growth trajectory.
analytics.tipCalendarIntegrations: Total number of calendar provider connections (Google, GHL, Calendly, etc.) across all users.
analytics.tipProductAdoption: Percentage of users who have each product active. Higher adoption = more popular product.

settings.slackRemoveConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove the Slack webhook?
settings.myPlanSubtitle: View your current subscription plan and included features.
settings.noProductsAssignedDesc: You don't have any products assigned yet. Browse the catalog below or contact your administrator.
settings.browseCatalogDesc: Browse available products and add them to your account.
settings.noPlanAssignedDesc: You don't have a plan assigned yet. Contact your administrator for more information.
settings.billingSubtitle: Your current call rates and billing information.
settings.billingInfoDesc: Calls are billed per minute based on your account rates. Contact your administrator to adjust rates.
settings.loadCreditsDesc: Contact your administrator to add credits.
settings.contactAdminCredits: Credit purchasing is not available yet. Contact your administrator.
settings.selectAmount: Select an amount or enter a custom value
settings.brandingSubtitle: Customize your platform appearance with your company logo and name.
settings.brandingInheritedAgency: This branding is set by your agency. Contact them to make changes.
settings.brandingOnlyOwners: Only account owners and agencies can customize branding.
settings.logoHelp: Enter a URL to your company logo. Recommended size: 40x40px or larger square image.
settings.calendarSubtitle: Connect your calendar providers to enable booking features in your AI agents.
settings.ghlDesc: Connect your GoHighLevel accounts for calendar booking features.
settings.ghlBearerHelp: Paste your Private Integration Token from GHL Settings > Integrations > Private Integrations.
settings.googleDesc: Connect your Google Calendar to check availability and book appointments.
settings.calendlyDesc: Connect Calendly to use your event types for scheduling.
settings.hubspotDesc: Connect HubSpot meetings for scheduling and booking.
settings.calcomDesc: Connect Cal.com with your API key for scheduling.
settings.calcomHelp: Find your API key at cal.com/settings/developer/api-keys
settings.apiKeysAdminOnly: Only account owners and admin team members can manage API keys.
settings.accountVapiKeysDesc: Configure VAPI keys for this account. These override the global platform keys and are encrypted securely.
settings.platformApiKeysDesc: Global API keys used across all accounts. Only the platform owner can manage these.
settings.apiKeysSubtitle: Configure your platform API keys for VAPI and OpenAI. These keys are encrypted and stored securely.
settings.vapiApiKeyDesc: Used for creating and managing voice AI agents.
settings.vapiPublicKeyDesc: Used for browser-based agent test calls. Find it in your VAPI dashboard under Account > Public Key.
settings.openaiApiKeyDesc: Used for the AI prompt generator feature.
settings.elevenLabsApiKeyDesc: Required for adding custom voices from the ElevenLabs Voice Library. Get your key at elevenlabs.io > Profile + API Key.
settings.anthropicApiKeyDesc: Used by the Reports feature to generate analyses with Claude. Create a key at console.anthropic.com.
settings.triggerApiKeyDesc: Required for the /api/call/trigger endpoint. External systems (Make.com, Zapier, GHL) must send this key in the x-api-key header to trigger outbound calls.
settings.triggerKeyCopyWarning: Copy this key now. It will not be shown again.
settings.triggerKeyReplaceConfirm: This will replace your current trigger API key. Any integrations using the old key will stop working. Continue?
settings.addTeamMembersDesc: Add team members to give others access to your account.
settings.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This cannot be undone.
settings.vapiPoolSubtitle: Add VAPI organization key pairs to the pool. Keys are automatically assigned to new accounts and released when accounts are deleted.
settings.vapiPoolEmptyDesc: Add VAPI key pairs to automatically assign them to new accounts.
settings.vapiPoolRemoveConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this key pair from the pool?
settings.vapiPoolCannotRemoveAssigned: Cannot remove a key that is assigned to an account
settings.complianceSubtitle: Manage HIPAA compliance settings, BAA tracking, and audit logs for your account.
settings.hipaaPrivacyRuleDesc: Protects the privacy of individually identifiable health information (PHI). Establishes standards for when and how PHI may be used or disclosed.
settings.hipaaSecurityRuleDesc: Sets national standards for protecting electronic PHI (ePHI). Requires administrative, physical, and technical safeguards.
settings.hipaaBreachNotificationDesc: Requires covered entities to notify affected individuals, HHS, and in some cases the media, following a breach of unsecured PHI.
settings.hipaaEnforcementDesc: Contains provisions relating to compliance and investigations, penalties for violations, and procedures for hearings.
settings.baaTemplateDesc: A standard Business Associate Agreement template for HIPAA compliance. Review and customize before use.
settings.baaTemplate:
BUSINESS ASSOCIATE AGREEMENT

This Business Associate Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [DATE] (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

[COVERED ENTITY NAME] ("Covered Entity")
and
[BUSINESS ASSOCIATE NAME] ("Business Associate")

1. DEFINITIONS
Terms used but not otherwise defined in this Agreement shall have the same meaning as those terms defined in 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164.

(a) "Breach" shall mean the acquisition, access, use, or disclosure of Protected Health Information (PHI) in a manner not permitted under the HIPAA Privacy Rule.
(b) "Protected Health Information" or "PHI" shall have the same meaning as the term "protected health information" in 45 CFR 160.103.
(c) "Required by Law" shall have the same meaning as the term "required by law" in 45 CFR 164.103.
(d) "Secretary" shall mean the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services or his/her designee.

2. OBLIGATIONS AND ACTIVITIES OF BUSINESS ASSOCIATE
Business Associate agrees to:

(a) Not use or disclose PHI other than as permitted or required by this Agreement or as required by law.
(b) Use appropriate safeguards and comply with the HIPAA Security Rule to prevent use or disclosure of PHI other than as provided for by this Agreement.
(c) Report to Covered Entity any use or disclosure of PHI not provided for by this Agreement of which it becomes aware, including any Breach of Unsecured PHI.
(d) In accordance with 45 CFR 164.502(e)(1)(ii), ensure that any subcontractors that create, receive, maintain, or transmit PHI on behalf of the Business Associate agree to the same restrictions and conditions.
(e) Make available PHI in a Designated Record Set to the Covered Entity or the Individual as required under 45 CFR 164.524.
(f) Make PHI available for amendment and incorporate any amendments to PHI as required under 45 CFR 164.526.
(g) Make available the information required to provide an accounting of disclosures as required under 45 CFR 164.528.
(h) Make its internal practices, books, and records relating to the use and disclosure of PHI available to the Secretary for purposes of determining compliance.

3. PERMITTED USES AND DISCLOSURES BY BUSINESS ASSOCIATE

(a) Business Associate may use or disclose PHI to perform functions, activities, or services for, or on behalf of, Covered Entity as specified in this Agreement, provided that such use or disclosure would not violate the HIPAA Privacy Rule.
(b) Business Associate may use PHI for the proper management and administration of the Business Associate or to carry out the legal responsibilities of the Business Associate.

4. TERM AND TERMINATION

(a) Term. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall terminate when all PHI is destroyed or returned to the Covered Entity.
(b) Termination for Cause. Upon Covered Entity's knowledge of a material breach by Business Associate, Covered Entity shall provide an opportunity for Business Associate to cure the breach. If cure is not possible, Covered Entity may terminate this Agreement.
(c) Effect of Termination. Upon termination, Business Associate shall return or destroy all PHI received from, or created or received by Business Associate on behalf of, Covered Entity.

5. MISCELLANEOUS

(a) This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state of [STATE].
(b) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.
(c) This Agreement may be amended only in writing signed by both parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

______________________________
[COVERED ENTITY NAME]
By: ___________________________
Title: _________________________
Date: _________________________

______________________________
[BUSINESS ASSOCIATE NAME]
By: ___________________________
Title: _________________________
Date: _________________________

chat.title: AI Assistant
chat.emptyState: Ask me anything about your agents or the platform!

agentEdit.notFoundDesc: This agent doesn't exist or you don't have access to it.
agentEdit.promptPlaceholder: Enter the system prompt for this agent...
agentEdit.firstMessageRequired: First message is required. Your agent needs an opening message to start the call.
agentEdit.firstMessagePlaceholder: Hello! How can I help you today?
agentEdit.firstMessageOutboundHint: Used only when this agent makes outbound calls. Leave empty to reuse the first message above.
agentEdit.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this agent? This action cannot be undone.
agentEdit.shareVoiceDesc: Generate a public link your client can open to talk to the agent in their browser, no login required. Voice is metered, so the per-call duration cap and per-IP daily limit are enforced strictly.
agentEdit.shareRegenerateConfirm: Regenerate the link? The old URL will stop working immediately.
agentEdit.startCallPrompt: Start a call to see the transcript
agentEdit.structuredDataDesc: Extract structured data from conversations using a JSON schema.
agentEdit.promptGeneratorDesc: Describe what your agent should do and AI will generate a prompt.
agentEdit.pasteVoiceId: Paste voice ID or ElevenLabs URL
agentEdit.noCallsYet: No calls yet for this agent
agentEdit.addToolsDesc: Add functions, call transfers, or other tools
agentEdit.stopSpeakingDesc: Configure how the assistant detects when the user starts speaking and stops its own speech. This helps create more natural conversations.
agentEdit.startSpeakingDesc: Configure when the assistant starts speaking after the user stops. Adjust timing, smart endpointing, and transcription-based detection for natural turn-taking.
agentEdit.callTimeoutsDesc: Configure maximum call duration and silence timeout. These help prevent runaway calls and wasted credits.
agentEdit.wordsBeforeStop: Words the user must speak before the assistant stops talking
agentEdit.voiceActivityDuration: Duration of voice activity needed to trigger stop
agentEdit.backoffCooldown: Cooldown after assistant stops before it can be interrupted again
agentEdit.waitSecondsDesc: Final delay before the assistant speaks after processing completes (default: 0.4s)
agentEdit.smartEndpointingDesc: Uses AI to predict when users finish speaking. English only.
agentEdit.onPunctuationDesc: Wait time after punctuation is detected (default: 0.1s)
agentEdit.onNoPunctuationDesc: Wait time when no punctuation is present (default: 1.5s)
agentEdit.onNumberDesc: Wait time after numbers are detected (default: 0.5s)
agentEdit.maxCallDurationDesc: Maximum duration before the call is automatically ended
agentEdit.silenceTimeoutDesc: End the call after this many seconds of silence
agentEdit.receivesCallData: Receives call data after each call ends
agentEdit.structuredDataFullDesc: Extract structured information from calls using a JSON schema. The AI will populate the schema fields based on conversation data.
agentEdit.jsonSchemaPlaceholder: Define the JSON schema for data to extract from the call
agentEdit.extractionInstructionsDesc: Instructions for how to extract data from the conversation
agentEdit.tipAiProvider: The AI provider that powers your agent's intelligence. OpenAI, Google, Anthropic and others are supported. Each provider has different models with varying speed, quality and cost.
agentEdit.tipLlmModel: The specific AI model to use. Larger models are smarter but slower and more expensive. Smaller models respond faster and cost less. The latency and cost per minute are shown for each option.
agentEdit.tipFirstMessage: The first thing your agent says when the call starts. For outbound calls, this is typically a greeting like "Hi, is this John?". For inbound calls, something like "Thank you for calling, how can I help you?"
agentEdit.tipCalendarOptions: Connect a calendar so your agent can check availability and book appointments during calls. Supports GoHighLevel, Google Calendar, Calendly, HubSpot and Cal.com.
agentEdit.tipCallTransfer: Enable your agent to transfer calls to a live person when needed. Configure a phone number, SIP address, or another assistant as the transfer destination.
agentEdit.tipAdvancedOptions: Fine-tune your agent's behavior: voice settings (stability, speed, similarity), transcriber configuration, background sounds, end-of-call messages, max call duration and custom tools/API integrations.
agentEdit.tipTranscriberProvider: The speech-to-text (STT) engine that converts the caller's voice into text for the AI to process. Each provider has different accuracy, speed and language support. Deepgram is recommended for the best balance of speed and accuracy.
agentEdit.tipTranscriberLanguage: The language the transcriber will listen for. Select "Multi" for automatic language detection if your agent handles calls in multiple languages, or pick a specific language for better accuracy.
agentEdit.promptDescribeAgent: Describe what your agent should do:
agentEdit.promptDescribeChanges: Describe the changes you want to make:
agentEdit.promptPlaceholderGenerate: Describe your business, what the agent should do, target audience, qualifying questions, desired outcome (booking, transfer, etc). E.g. "A dental clinic called Bright Smiles. The agent should call leads who filled out a form, ask about their dental needs, qualify them, and book a consultation appointment..."
agentEdit.promptPlaceholderUpdate: Describe the changes you want to make to the current prompt. E.g. "Change the agent name to Sarah", "Add a question about budget", "Make the tone more formal"...
agentEdit.promptPlaceholderGenerateEs: Describe tu negocio, qué debe hacer el agente, público objetivo, preguntas de calificación, resultado deseado (agendar cita, transferir, etc). Ej: "Una clínica dental llamada Bright Smiles. El agente debe llamar a leads que llenaron un formulario, preguntar sobre sus necesidades dentales, calificarlos y agendar una cita de consulta..."
agentEdit.promptPlaceholderUpdateEs: Describe los cambios que quieres hacer al prompt actual. Ej: "Cambiar el nombre del agente a Maria", "Agregar una pregunta sobre el presupuesto", "Cambiar el tono a mas formal"...
agentEdit.wizGoalsPlaceholder: Describe the main goals for this agent. E.g. Qualify leads and book consultations for our dental clinic...
agentEdit.wizGoalsPlaceholderEs: Describe los objetivos principales de este agente. Ej: Calificar leads y agendar consultas para nuestra clinica dental...
agentEdit.wizQualifyingQuestionsPlaceholder: List the questions the agent should ask to qualify leads...
agentEdit.wizCommonObjectionsPlaceholder: List common objections and how to handle them...
agentEdit.wizCommonIssuesPlaceholder: List common support issues the agent should handle...
agentEdit.wizEscalationRulesPlaceholder: Describe when and how to escalate to a human...
agentEdit.wizServicesOfferedPlaceholder: List the services available for booking...
agentEdit.wizAvailabilityNotesPlaceholder: Describe availability hours, booking rules, etc...
agentEdit.wizSurveyQuestionsPlaceholder: List the survey questions the agent should ask...
agentEdit.wizAdditionalNotesPlaceholder: Any extra context or instructions for the AI...
agentEdit.waitForResponseDesc: When enabled, the AI waits for the API response before continuing. Disable for fire-and-forget requests.
agentEdit.noResponseYet: Send a test request to see the response here
agentEdit.tipVoiceModel: Choose the ElevenLabs voice model. Multilingual v2 offers the best quality and language support. Flash models have lower latency. Turbo models balance speed and quality.
agentEdit.tipVoiceTuning: Fine-tune your agent's voice characteristics. Adjust stability (consistency vs expressiveness), clarity, speaking speed, and style exaggeration to match your desired persona.
agentEdit.tipBgSound: Add ambient background noise to make conversations feel more natural. The office option simulates a realistic call center environment.
agentEdit.tipAgentTools: Add custom API integrations and functions your agent can call during conversations. Use tools for booking appointments, looking up data, or triggering external workflows.
agentEdit.tipWebhook: Receive a POST request with full call data (transcript, duration, outcome) when each call ends. Useful for CRM updates, analytics, or triggering follow-up workflows.
agentEdit.tipStructuredData: Automatically extract structured information from conversations using a JSON schema. The AI populates fields like customer name, appointment date, or issue type based on what was discussed.
agentEdit.tipVoicemail: When enabled, the agent automatically detects answering machines and voicemail greetings, allowing it to leave a message or end the call gracefully.
agentEdit.ghlCrmTagMappingDesc: Assign GHL tags to contacts based on each call outcome.
agentEdit.ghlCrmDeleteOldTags: Remove previous outcome tags before applying new ones
agentEdit.ghlCrmPipelineMappingDesc: Create or move opportunities in a GHL pipeline based on call outcome.
agentEdit.ghlCrmCustomFieldsDesc: Add GHL custom fields to extract from calls. Select a field and describe what to extract.
agentEdit.ghlCrmNoCustomFields: No custom fields found in your GHL location.
agentEdit.ghlCrmAssignUserDesc: Assign a GHL user to the opportunity for this stage
agentEdit.ghlCrmNoteDesc: Create a note on the contact when this stage is triggered
agentEdit.callbacksDesc: Allow the AI agent to schedule a callback when the customer asks to be called back later.
agentEdit.callbacksInfo: When enabled, the agent can schedule a callback during a call. A background process will automatically initiate the outbound call at the scheduled time using this agent.
agentEdit.callbacksPhoneNote: The agent's assigned phone number will be used for callbacks. Make sure a phone number is assigned to this agent.
agentEdit.followUpsDesc: Automatically schedule follow-up calls based on call outcome.
agentEdit.followUpsInfo: When enabled, the system will automatically schedule follow-up calls after a call ends, based on the outcome. A background process fires the follow-up at the configured interval. The agent receives context about the previous conversation when appropriate.
agentEdit.followUpsContextNote: For 'answered' and 'not interested' outcomes, the agent will receive context about the previous conversation. For 'failed', 'voicemail', and 'unknown', the call is treated as a first attempt since the customer wasn't reached.
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerDesc: Automatically trigger a chatbot after the call ends to send follow-up messages.
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerInfo: When enabled, a linked chatbot will be triggered after the call with context about the conversation (summary, outcome, customer number). The chatbot's workflow handles the actual messaging (WhatsApp, SMS, etc.).
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerNoChatbots: No chatbots found. Create a chatbot first.
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerByStructuredDataDesc: Trigger when a data field matches a value
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerDelayDesc: 0 = immediate, or set minutes to wait before triggering
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerMessagePlaceholder: Leave empty for auto-generated context. Use {{customerNumber}}, {{outcome}}, {{summary}}, {{agentName}} as variables.
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerMessageDesc: Override the default context message sent to the chatbot.
agentEdit.callToolDesc: Allow the chatbot to trigger outbound voice calls using an AI agent.
agentEdit.callToolInfo: When enabled, the chatbot can call a customer immediately or schedule a call for later. Two tools are auto-generated: one for immediate calls and one for scheduled callbacks.
agentEdit.callToolPhoneNote: This phone number will be used as the caller ID for outbound calls.
agentEdit.stopSpeakingSubtitle: When to stop on user interruption
agentEdit.webhookCardDesc: Send call data to external URL
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerCardDesc: Trigger a chatbot after the call
agentEdit.webhookSubtitle: Send call data to your server endpoint
agentEdit.ghlCrmSubtitle: Sync call outcomes with GoHighLevel CRM
agentEdit.followUpsSubtitle: Automatically retry calls based on outcomes
agentEdit.chatbotTriggerSubtitle: Trigger a chatbot conversation after the call
agentEdit.calendarSubtitle: Configure booking integration for this agent
agentEdit.contactIdHelp: Uses this contact directly instead of searching by email.
agentEdit.requiredContactDataDesc: What the agent must collect before booking.
agentEdit.noCalendarsFound: No calendars found for this account.
agentEdit.providerNotConnected: This provider is not connected yet.
agentEdit.agentDescPlaceholder: Short description of this agent's purpose...
agentEdit.toolTestEmpty: Add body parameters above to fill in test values here.
agentEdit.variablesDesc: Define extra variables to pass via the API trigger...
agentEdit.paramApiKey: Your trigger API key (from Settings > API Keys)
agentEdit.paramClientId: Unique client identifier (used for follow-up tracking)
agentEdit.paramFrom: Your Twilio phone number assigned to this agent
agentEdit.paramTo: The customer's phone number to call
agentEdit.paramOverrides: Optional runtime overrides for agent settings
agentEdit.toolRequiredFields: Name, description, and URL are required
agentEdit.greetingPlaceholder: Hello! How can I help you today?
agentEdit.structuredDataPromptPlaceholder: Extract the customer's name, appointment date they requested, and the issue they described...
agentEdit.calendarScenarioPlaceholder: e.g., Use when customer wants a sales demo
agentEdit.transferScenarioPlaceholder: e.g., When customer wants to speak with a human agent
agentEdit.transferMsgPlaceholder: e.g., Let me connect you now, please hold...
agentEdit.transferEntryScenarioPlaceholder: e.g., When customer wants to speak with sales
agentEdit.transferEntryMsgPlaceholder: e.g., Let me connect you now...
agentEdit.failedLoadGhlData: Failed to load GHL data. Please check your GHL connection in Settings.
agentEdit.callSelectPhoneAndNumber: Please select a phone number and enter customer number
agentEdit.callAgentNotSynced: Agent is not synced. Please save the agent first.
agentEdit.callPhoneNotSynced: Selected phone number is not synced.

scheduledCalls.title: Scheduled Calls
scheduledCalls.subtitle: View and manage scheduled callbacks and follow-ups
scheduledCalls.noCallbacksDesc: No scheduled callbacks yet. Callbacks are created when a customer requests to be called back.
scheduledCalls.noFollowUpsDesc: No scheduled follow-ups yet. Follow-ups are automatically created based on call outcomes.
scheduledCalls.confirmCancel: Are you sure you want to cancel this scheduled call?
scheduledCalls.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this scheduled call? This action cannot be undone.

pricing.modelRatesDesc: Cost per minute for each AI model. This rate is combined with the transcriber rate to calculate total call cost.
pricing.transcriberRatesDesc: Cost per minute for each speech-to-text transcriber provider.
pricing.howItWorksDesc: Each call is billed based on the AI model and transcriber used by the agent. Total cost per minute = model rate + transcriber rate. If no dynamic pricing is configured, legacy per-account rates apply.
pricing.agencyInfo: You can override global rates for your clients. Leave a field empty to use the global default. Highlighted items have overrides set.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Create and track support tickets
support.noTicketsDesc: You haven't created any support tickets yet.
support.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this ticket? This cannot be undone.

training.title: Training
training.subtitle: Video tutorials to help you get the most out of the platform
training.vid1Desc: Learn how to navigate the dashboard, understand the main sections, and set up your account for the first time.
training.vid2Desc: Tour of the sidebar, overview stats, quick actions, and how to switch between dark and light mode.
training.vid3Desc: Step-by-step guide to set up an outbound calling agent with custom prompts, voice selection, and LLM model configuration.
training.vid4Desc: Configure an inbound agent to handle incoming calls, assign phone numbers, and set up call routing rules.
training.vid5Desc: Choose and customize AI voices from ElevenLabs, OpenAI, or PlayHT. Configure transcription providers and multi-language support.
training.vid6Desc: Use the AI-powered prompt wizard to generate optimized system prompts for your voice agents step by step.
training.vid7Desc: Add custom API tools to your agents, configure structured data extraction, chatbot triggers, and scheduled callbacks/follow-ups.
training.vid8Desc: Build text-based chatbots with custom prompts, model selection, and webhook output configuration.
training.vid9Desc: Add booking tools, phone call initiation, and custom API request tools to your chatbots for powerful automations.
training.vid10Desc: Set up post-call chatbot triggers to automatically send follow-up messages via GHL SMS, WhatsApp, or webhook after a voice call ends.
training.vid11Desc: Connect your Twilio account, import phone numbers, and assign them to voice agents for inbound and outbound calls.
training.vid12Desc: Connect Google Calendar, GoHighLevel, Calendly, HubSpot, or Cal.com to let your agents and chatbots book appointments.
training.vid13Desc: Use the trigger API to initiate calls programmatically, configure webhooks for call events, and integrate with external systems.
training.vid14Desc: View call volume charts, outcome distribution, cost analysis, agent performance, and revenue tracking with custom date ranges.
training.vid15Desc: Browse call history, filter by date/agent/outcome, view transcripts, listen to recordings, and export call data.
training.vid16Desc: Configure automatic callback scheduling and multi-attempt follow-up calls based on call outcomes.
training.vid17Desc: Add team members to your account, assign admin or user roles, and manage team access and permissions.
training.vid18Desc: Create and manage client sub-accounts, set feature flags, configure per-account billing, and manage agency relationships.
training.vid19Desc: Generate shareable read-only portal links for clients to view their sessions, recordings, and summaries without logging in.
training.vid20Desc: Understand the credit system, add funds, manage products and subscriptions, configure call rates, and track payment history.
training.vid21Desc: Configure VAPI keys, trigger API keys, Twilio credentials, Slack integration, and general account preferences.
training.vid22Desc: Customize your platform with your company name, logo, tagline, and branded experience for your clients.
training.vid23Desc: Enable HIPAA compliance, configure data retention policies, manage BAA documents, and review audit trail logs.

payments.title: Payments
payments.subtitle: Manage products and user subscriptions
payments.noUserProducts: No products assigned to users yet
payments.confirmDeleteProduct: Are you sure you want to delete this product?
payments.confirmRemoveProduct: Are you sure you want to remove this product from the user?
payments.clientRedirect: Visit Settings > Account to view your products and browse the catalog.
payments.changePlanDesc: Select a new billing cycle for this product.
payments.confirmCancelProduct: Are you sure you want to cancel this product? This action cannot be undone.
payments.comingSoonDesc: This product will be available soon. Stay tuned!

chatbotEdit.credentialPickerHint: Pick a saved credential or leave inline. Manage in
chatbotEdit.vectorStoreDesc: Lets the agent semantically retrieve documents via your Supabase pgvector match function.
chatbotEdit.vectorStoreFooter: Expects a Supabase RPC with signature `match_documents(query_embedding vector, match_threshold float, match_count int)` returning rows with `id`, `content`, and `similarity`. Embeddings are generated server-side with text-embedding-3-small.
chatbotEdit.shareDesc: Generate a public link your client can open to chat with the bot, no login required. The bot still uses your credits, so we cap usage daily and per IP.
chatbotEdit.shareRegenerateConfirm: Regenerate the link? The old URL will stop working immediately.
chatbotEdit.webhookDesc: Send a POST request to this endpoint to interact with your chatbot.
chatbotEdit.uniqueSessionId: Unique session ID for conversation memory
chatbotEdit.dynamicVariables: Dynamic variables to inject into the prompt
chatbotEdit.variablesDesc: Define variables that can be passed via the webhook and used as placeholders in the system prompt with
chatbotEdit.name: Name
chatbotEdit.webhookForwardingDesc: Forward the chatbot's response to an external webhook URL. The response will be sent as a POST request to this URL.
chatbotEdit.responsesForwarded: Responses will be forwarded to this URL
chatbotEdit.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a helpful customer support chatbot...
chatbotEdit.noCalendarsFound: No calendars found for this account.
chatbotEdit.providerNotConnected: This provider is not connected yet.
chatbotEdit.ghlTestContactIdDesc: GHL Contact ID used when testing. In production, the session ID is used as the contact ID.
chatbotEdit.ghlTestContactNameDesc: Contact name used when testing the chatbot.
chatbotEdit.scenarioPlaceholder: e.g., Use when customer wants a sales demo
chatbotEdit.callToolDesc: Trigger outbound voice calls using an AI agent.
chatbotEdit.noGoogleAccounts: No Google accounts connected. Connect one in Calendar settings first.
chatbotEdit.ghlCrmContactIdNote: contactId is auto-injected from conversation context.
chatbotEdit.whenToUsePlaceholder: e.g. When lead qualifies for premium plan
chatbotEdit.noteInstructionPlaceholder: e.g. Summarize why the lead is interested and key details from the conversation
chatbotEdit.cfInstructionPlaceholder: e.g. Set to the lead's reported budget range
chatbotEdit.describeChanges: Describe the changes you want to make:
chatbotEdit.describeChangesPlaceholderEs: Describe los cambios que quieres hacer al prompt actual...
chatbotEdit.describeChangesPlaceholderEn: Describe the changes you want to make to the current prompt...
chatbotEdit.goalsPlaceholderEs: Describe los objetivos principales de este chatbot...
chatbotEdit.goalsPlaceholderEn: Describe the main goals for this chatbot. E.g. Answer billing questions, help with password resets...
chatbotEdit.qualifyingQuestionsPlaceholder: List the questions the chatbot should ask to qualify leads...
chatbotEdit.commonObjectionsPlaceholder: List common objections and how to handle them...
chatbotEdit.commonIssuesPlaceholder: List common support issues the chatbot should handle...
chatbotEdit.escalationRulesPlaceholder: Describe when and how to escalate to a human...
chatbotEdit.servicesOfferedPlaceholder: List the services available for booking...
chatbotEdit.availabilityNotesPlaceholder: Describe availability hours, booking rules, etc...
chatbotEdit.surveyQuestionsPlaceholder: List the survey questions the chatbot should ask...
chatbotEdit.additionalNotesPlaceholder: Any extra context or instructions for the AI...
chatbotEdit.followUpRulesDesc: Automatically trigger actions when conversations go inactive or GHL opportunities stall in a stage.
chatbotEdit.oppInStage: Opportunity in stage for X days
chatbotEdit.aiMessageDesc: AI will read the conversation history and generate a contextual follow-up message.
chatbotEdit.aiInstructionsPlaceholder: Optional instructions for AI, e.g.: Be friendly, mention our 20% discount, try to re-engage...
chatbotEdit.messageBatchingDesc: When enabled, incoming messages are buffered for the specified duration. If another message arrives during the wait, the timer resets. All buffered messages are merged into a single request to n8n, so the AI responds once instead of per-message.
chatbotEdit.messageBatchingWarning: When buffering is active, the webhook returns {"queued": true} immediately instead of the n8n response. The merged message is forwarded after the delay.

reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Generate analyses of your calls and chatbot conversations using Claude.
reports.noneYetDesc: Create your first report to summarize and analyze your data.
reports.name: Name
reports.newSubtitle: Pick a dataset, set filters, write your prompt, and Claude will generate the report.
reports.promptPlaceholder: Describe what you want from the report. e.g. 'Summarize the most common reasons customers declined the offer, with quoted examples and a count for each reason.'
